Plaintif Motion To Compel Response Of Defendant Whole Foods Market, California, Inc. To First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Request For Production; And Request For Sanctions.
Off Calendar Jul-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Aparma L. Reddy, Esq., and D. Joshua Voorhees, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Russell Brimer
Matthew s. Kenefick, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Whole Foods Market, California, Inc., Whole Foods Market, Inc., and Herman Dodge & Sons, Inc.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling ISSUED 7/12/07 was adopted:
Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Response of Defendant Whole Foods Market, California, Inc. to First Set of Special Interrogatories, Request For Production; and Request For Sanctions denied without prejudice to plaintiff propounding further discovery limited in scope to specific products purchased from defendant Herman Dodge & Sons, Inc. by defendant Whole Foods Market, California, Inc. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT.

MINI MINUTES: ROSS-DEFENDANT RUSSELL BRIMER'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: APARNA L. REDDY, ESQUIRE (HIRST & CHABLER, 510.848.8880) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MICHAEL J. HASSEN, ESQUIRE (JEFFERS MANGELS BUTLER & MARMARO, 415.398.8080) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT PLAINTIFF HAS SOUGHT ANY PARTICULAR AMOUNT OF FEES AS TO WHICH THERE COULD BE AN EXISTING CONTROVERSY. THE ISSUES RAISED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT CONCERNING THE AMOUNT OF FEES CAN ALL BE ADDRESSED IN ANY MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF FEES THAT IS ULTIMATELY MADE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER.
